label: plain_text
text:
The
Lebombo
Bone
discovered
in
the
Lebombo
Mountains
between
South
Africa
and
Swaziland,
dates
back
approximately
35,000
years. It is a bone
e featuring
29
distinct, deliberately-carved
uniformly-sized notches. Anthropologists and mathematicians believe
this was not just random scratching, but a tool used as a lunar
phase
counter or a menstrual calendar, indicating
that early
humans
swere
tracking
g time through natural numbers.
